skill.name: gws-drive
skill.description: Google Drive: Manage files, folders, and shared drives via the Google Workspace CLI.
skill.summary: A skill that enables Google Drive file management through the gws command-line interface, supporting all Drive API v3 operations including files, folders, permissions, and shared drives.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 193 patterns across 1 file, but all are FALSE POSITIVES. The SKILL.md file is documentation for the Google Workspace CLI (gws), not executable code. External command references are CLI usage examples, URLs are documentation links to developers.google.com, and path references are legitimate relative paths to other skill files. No malicious intent detected.

risk_factor_evidence.reasoning: The gws command references are documentation examples showing CLI usage syntax, not actual shell execution code. This is a skill definition file that describes available commands.
All detected URLs are hyperlinks to official Google Developers documentation (developers.google.com). These are reference links for users, not hardcoded API endpoints for data exfiltration.
The ../ paths are legitimate relative references to prerequisite skill documentation files (gws-shared/SKILL.md and gws-drive-upload/SKILL.md), not path traversal attacks.

low_findings.title: External CLI Dependency
low_findings.description: Skill requires the gws (Google Workspace CLI) binary to be installed on the host system. The skill itself is documentation that describes CLI commands rather than implementing direct API calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The metadata section explicitly declares 'bins: gws' as a required dependency, which is standard for CLI wrapper skills.

content.user_title: Manage Google Drive files and folders
content.value_statement: Organize and collaborate on Drive content without leaving your AI assistant. Execute Google Drive API operations through natural language commands using the Google Workspace CLI.
actual_capabilities: List, create, copy, update, and delete files in Google Drive
Manage file permissions and sharing settings
Create and manage shared drives (formerly Team Drives)
Add, list, and manage comments and replies on files
Export Google Workspace documents to various formats
limitations: Requires the gws (Google Workspace CLI) binary to be installed and configured
Requires valid Google Workspace authentication and API access
Cannot directly upload file content - must use gws-drive-upload helper skill
Google Doc exports are limited to 10 MB

use_cases.title: Automated file organization
use_cases.description: Create folder structures, move files between directories, and organize Drive content programmatically through natural language commands.
use_cases.target_user: Operations managers handling large document repositories

use_cases.title: Collaborative access management
use_cases.description: Grant, review, and revoke file permissions for team members. Manage comment threads and approval workflows on sensitive documents.

use_cases.title: Shared drive administration
use_cases.description: Create shared drives for departments, configure visibility settings, and manage drive membership and metadata.
use_cases.target_user: IT administrators overseeing Google Workspace deployments

prompt_templates.title: List recent files
prompt_templates.prompt: List my 10 most recently modified files in Google Drive, showing their names, IDs, and last modified dates.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick overview of recent work activity

prompt_templates.title: Create folder structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new folder called "Q1 2026 Reports" in my Drive, then create three subfolders inside it: "January", "February", and "March".

prompt_templates.title: Share file with team
prompt_templates.prompt: Share the file named "Budget Proposal.xlsx" with alice@company.com and bob@company.com, giving them editor access. Add a note saying "Please review by Friday".

prompt_templates.title: Export and organize documents
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all Google Docs in the "Project Alpha" folder, export each as PDF format, and list their export status. Then show me which files were successfully exported.

output_examples.input: List files in the root of my Drive
output_examples.output:
Found 5 files:
- Q4 Report.docx (ID: 1abc123) - Modified: 2026-03-20
- Budget.xlsx (ID: 2def456) - Modified: 2026-03-18
- Team Photo.png (ID: 3ghi789) - Modified: 2026-03-15
- Project Plan.gsheet (ID: 4jkl012) - Modified: 2026-03-10
- Meeting Notes.gdoc (ID: 5mno345) - Modified: 2026-03-05

Successfully created folder:
- Name: Client Deliverables
- ID: 6pqr678
- Location: My Drive
- Created: 2026-03-24T08:30:00Z

best_practices: Always inspect command schemas with 'gws schema drive.resource.method' before executing to understand required parameters
Use the 'fields' parameter to request only needed data, improving performance and reducing response size
Set 'trashed=false' when listing files to exclude deleted items from results
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to upload files directly - use the gws-drive-upload helper skill instead
Avoid listing revisions on frequently-edited Google Docs - results may be incomplete for large revision histories
Do not perform concurrent permission updates on the same file - only the last update will be applied

faq.question: What authentication is required to use this skill?
faq.answer: You must have the gws (Google Workspace CLI) binary installed and authenticated with your Google Workspace account. See the gws-shared skill for authentication setup instructions.

faq.question: Can this skill upload new files to Drive?
faq.answer: No, this skill handles metadata operations only. For file uploads, use the gws-drive-upload helper skill which handles multipart upload metadata automatically.

faq.question: How do I share a file with someone outside my organization?
faq.answer: Use the permissions.create method with the email address and role. External sharing must be enabled by your Google Workspace administrator.

faq.question: What is the difference between 'drives' and 'teamdrives' resources?
faq.answer: Teamdrives is deprecated. All operations should use the 'drives' resource, which provides the same functionality with updated naming (shared drives).

faq.question: Why can't I see all revisions of my Google Doc?
faq.answer: For frequently edited Google Docs, Sheets, and Slides, the revisions.list response may be incomplete. Older revisions might be omitted due to API limitations.

faq.question: How do I permanently delete a file?
faq.answer: First move the file to trash by updating its trashed property to true. Then permanently delete by calling files.delete on the trashed file.
